The following is the peer review of the project proposal by [name of team completing peer review]. The team members that participated in this review are 

- Song Yi Kim - @songyi30
- Drew Halloran - @DrewHalloran
- Usha gourigari - @Usha-Gourigari
- Rishinath Reddy - @Rishi-1482


- Describe the goal of the project.

The goal of this project is to analyze species distribution and conservation status across the 15 most visited U.S. National Parks. 

- Describe the data used or collected.

The data focuses on 15 most visited parks in the US and it is taken from National Park Service U.S Department of the Interior. The dataset is compiled using a variety of wildlife monitoring methods, including direct observation, field surveys, camera traps, acoustic monitoring, and citizen science programs. This dataset contains 61,119 records with 28 columns. The dataset has been updated multiple times, with the most recent update on September 2, 2024.

- Describe how the research question will be answered, e.g. what approaches / methods will be used.

Research Question 1 focuses on the variation in global conservation ranks across the 15 most visited U.S. national parks. To answer this, the analysis compares G_ranks with the number of species in each park.
A bar chart will be used to represent the average conservation rank across each park, with parks on the x-axis and rank values on the y-axis.
Another approach is using a thematic map to visually represent the distribution of conservation ranks across the parks.

Research Question 2 focuses on the distribution of species by category across 15 national parks. The analysis involves calculating the total number of species for each category in each park and comparing them across different parks.
For visualization, a stacked bar chart will be used, where parks are on the x-axis, and the y-axis represents the number of species in each category.

- Is there anything that is unclear from the proposal?

Providing individual question analysis plan rather than overall analysis plan and approach is unclear. 

- Provide constructive feedback on how the team might be able to improve their project.
  
Multiple conservation status variables (G_Rank, TEStatus, StateStatus, and S_Rank) are mentioned, but it is unclear how they will be integrated or compared.

- What aspect of this project are you most interested in and would like to see highlighted in the presentation.

The most interesting is which national parks have the highest number of species.

- Provide constructive feedback on any issues with file and/or code organization.

NA
